_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass (SWORD VARIANT) — take the L8 'Footman' composited keeper and produce the L9 'Knight-Sergeant' variant (Tier 3 top).
history: v1 (2026-05-07): generated by build_sword_prompts.py from `infantry_edit_L8_to_L9.json` (mace variant). Bulk language substitutions: mace→sword, wooden haft→leather grip, iron spikes→cross-guard, mace head→sword blade, etc. Weapon unchanged at this level — only PRESERVE / POSE language updated to reference the sword.
_meta.input_strategy: Within-tier chained (Rule 2): two inputs = L8 composite + L1_Sword_Base scale anchor.

prompt: CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION (READ FIRST) — The first input image is the canonical content reference. It already shows the L8 'Footman' kit accumulated on top of the L1 base. You MUST preserve every prior feature shown in the first input image: (a) the STEEL SALLET HELM dome and forward-projecting brim with brim rivets — the new L9 step EXTENDS the existing half-visor downward to a fully-closed visor and ADDS horns and a gold band, but the helm dome and brim are preserved; (b) the FULL STEEL CUIRASS with three articulated horizontal lames at the abdomen, a thicker medial ridge, and the L8 fine-line scrollwork engraving running vertically along the medial ridge — the cuirass and its engraving are preserved at L9; (c) the WIDE WARM-BROWN LEATHER WAIST BELT with square dark-iron buckle visible between the tassets at the front; (d) the LONGER 3-LAME STEEL TASSETS extending to mid-thigh with slight outward flare at the lower edge — the new L9 step ADDS gold filigree centered on each tasset, but the 3-lame articulation and overall shape are preserved; (e) the 3-LAME STEEL PAULDRONS on both shoulders (small upper disc + medium base disc + downward bicep lame) with the L8 GOLD RIVET HEADS at the seam between the upper disc and the base disc — the new L9 step ADDS gold filigree on the upper face of each pauldron, but the 3-lame structure is preserved; (f) the STEEL VAMBRACES on both forearms extending continuously into STEEL GAUNTLETS at the wrists with knuckle-plates and articulated finger-plates; (g) the SOLID FLANGED STEEL SWORD BLADE with 6 vertical flanges held in the gauntleted anatomical-RIGHT hand; (h) the SWORD LEATHER GRIP with leather grip wrap on the upper third and two L6 steel grip-rings at the top and bottom of the leather grip; (i) the LARGER STEEL-FACED ROUND SHIELD with the embossed sun-burst boss (raised central dome with 8 short triangular rays), the four iron cross strap-bands, and the iron rim band with ~16 dark-iron studs — the new L9 step REPLACES the iron rim band with a GOLD rim band and ADDS a gold ring around the central boss dome, but the round shape, the cross strap-bands, the studs, and the sun-burst rays are all preserved; (j) the STEEL SABATONS over warm-brown leather boots (toe caps and ankle straps); (k) the LIVERY SKIRT BAND visible through the narrow ~2-inch front gap between the two tassets — the red+mustard split tunic skirt band remains visible at L9 (livery transition to skirt-only band L8-L9); (l) the BROWN LEATHER HIP POUCH on anatomical-RIGHT hip and the BROWN LEATHER KNIFE SHEATH with cream bone-handle knife on anatomical-LEFT hip (visible at the upper edge of each tasset); (m) the MAIL COIF back-of-neck and shoulder drape behind/below the helm. DO NOT revert any of these features. The second input image is provided ONLY as a scale and framing reference — match its character-to-canvas proportions exactly; do NOT apply any of its content. EDIT GOAL — Apply FIVE visible additive upgrades to the infantryman shown in the first input image, marking the top of Tier 3 (Knight-Sergeant). The face becomes FULLY CONCEALED for the first time (closed visor); the helm gains horns and a gold band; the shield gains gold trim; the pauldrons and tassets gain gold filigree. Silhouette mightiness rule active: the new horns add vertical extent at the head silhouette. CRITICAL gold rendering note (read this carefully): every gold element at L9 MUST render as BUTTERY POLISHED YELLOW GOLD METAL with a clearly distinct warm-yellow hue contrasting strongly against the cool silver-gray steel surfaces. The gold should be SATURATED and IMMEDIATELY READABLE as gold at thumbnail scale — NOT brass, NOT bronze, NOT yellow paint, NOT mustard tint, NOT a warm-tinted highlight on steel. Render gold with: (i) a saturated buttery-yellow base tone (warm yellow-orange, like polished 18-karat gold, distinctly more yellow than the surrounding cool gray steel); (ii) a soft top-down highlight that reads almost white-yellow; (iii) a deeper warm shadow on the underside that reads honey-orange. Each gold element must be visually unmistakable as gold, not as a steel surface with warm lighting. (1) CLOSED VISOR EXTENDING THE L8 HALF-VISOR DOWNWARD TO FULLY CONCEAL THE FACE — extend the existing half-visor downward with a steel lower-visor plate to fully close the face. The closed visor now covers the entire face from forehead down to the top of the bevor (where the bevor meets the breastplate at the throat). The visor has a single horizontal eye slit at eye level (approximately 1 inch tall, 4 inches wide, dark/shadowed inside) and a row of small breathing perforations (approximately 5-7 small dark circular holes) running horizontally across the lower-visor section roughly at mouth level. The visor is cool silver-gray polished steel matching the helm body. THE FACE IS NOW FULLY CONCEALED — no warm peachy-tan skin visible anywhere above the bevor; no eyes visible (only the dark slit); no nose tip visible; no mustache visible; no beard visible; no chin visible; no hair visible. The character's identity above the bevor is now entirely the helm + visor silhouette. The visor has a soft three-dimensional contour with a subtle medial ridge running vertically down the center (matching the cuirass medial ridge). (2) MODEST UPWARD-CURVING STEEL HORNS RISING FROM THE HELM TEMPLES — add a pair of small upward-curving steel horns rising from the temple area on each side of the helm dome (above the ear position). Each horn is approximately 6 inches tall, sweeps slightly OUTWARD and slightly BACK from the helm body (not straight up), pointed at the tip, cool silver-gray polished steel matching the helm. The horns are tapered (wider at the base where they attach to the helm, narrowing to a sharp point at the tip). The horn bases attach with a small rounded gold mount at each temple (small buttery-gold disc where the horn meets the helm — this is part of the L9 gold language; the horn body itself is steel). The horns add visible vertical extent to the silhouette — the helm-plus-horns now reads taller than the L8 helm alone (silhouette mightiness rule). (3) BUTTERY GOLD BAND WRAPPING THE HELM CIRCUMFERENCE AT THE BRIM LINE — add a continuous BUTTERY POLISHED GOLD band wrapping the helm at the brim line all the way around (front to sides to back). The gold band is approximately 1 inch tall, sits at the seam between the helm dome (above) and the brim (below) at the front, and continues around the sides and back of the helm. The gold is saturated buttery yellow-gold with a soft top-down highlight and warm honey-orange shadow on the underside. The gold band must be IMMEDIATELY READABLE as gold metal at thumbnail scale, distinctly contrasting with the cool silver-gray steel of the helm body and the brim. The L8 dark-iron brim rivets remain visible AT THE BRIM (below the gold band). (4) BUTTERY GOLD SHIELD RIM BAND PLUS GOLD RING AROUND THE BOSS — REPLACE the iron rim band on the round shield (the dark-iron rim with ~16 studs from L7/L8) with a continuous BUTTERY POLISHED GOLD rim band wrapping the entire shield perimeter, approximately ½ inch wide, saturated buttery yellow-gold matching the helm band. The studs around the rim become small GOLD round rivet heads against the gold rim band (still ~16 studs visible, now gold). ADDITIONALLY add a thin GOLD RING around the central boss dome at the base of the boss where it meets the shield face — the gold ring is approximately ¼ inch thick, encircling the boss like a halo, distinctly buttery-gold against the cool silver-gray of the boss dome and the shield face. The boss dome itself remains cool silver-gray steel; the sun-burst rays remain cool silver-gray; the gold is ONLY at the boss's outer ring AND at the shield rim. The four iron cross strap-bands remain dark cool-gray iron (NOT gold at L9 — only the rim and the boss-ring are gold on the shield). (5) GOLD FILIGREE SCROLLWORK ON THE PAULDRONS AND TASSETS — add fine-line BUTTERY POLISHED GOLD filigree scrollwork etched onto the upper face of each pauldron (one motif centered on each pauldron's largest base disc) and centered on each tasset (one motif centered on each tasset's middle lame). The filigree motif is a stylized leaf-and-curl pattern, hand-painted in saturated buttery yellow-gold against the cool silver-gray steel surface, approximately 2 inches tall on each pauldron and 2.5 inches tall on each tasset. The gold filigree is etched-in / inlaid (not raised relief) — the lines themselves read as buttery-gold pen strokes against the steel. The cuirass medial ridge engraving from L8 remains tonal-only (no gold added to the cuirass at L9 — only pauldrons + tassets get gold filigree at this level). ===== PRESERVE FROM THE FIRST INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the first input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion: the helm dome, brim, brim rivets; the cuirass with its 3 horizontal abdominal lames and the tonal scrollwork engraving on the medial ridge; the layered 3-disc/lame pauldrons on both shoulders (small upper disc + base disc + downward bicep lame) with the L8 gold seam rivets remaining as-is; the steel vambraces extending into steel gauntlets with knuckle-plates and articulated finger-plates; the wide leather belt with square dark-iron buckle; the brown leather hip pouch on anatomical-RIGHT hip; the brown leather knife sheath with bone-handle knife on anatomical-LEFT hip; the longer 3-lame steel tassets with outward flare; the LIVERY skirt band visible through the front tasset gap; the dark blue / muted steel-blue trousers visible at the calf; the steel sabatons over warm-brown leather boots; the leather grip wrap on the sword; the two steel grip-rings on the sword leather grip; the sword leather grip (the sword grip remains wood; the sword blade is the L7 flanged steel head); the longsword steel blade with a deep central fuller; the larger steel-faced round shield with the central steel boss + sun-burst rays + four iron cross strap-bands + ~16 rim studs (now gold rivets per Edit Goal item 4); the mail coif back-of-neck and shoulder drape behind/below the helm. ===== POSE (must match the first input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees to the viewer's left, weight planted evenly on both feet with slight forward lean. THE SWORD ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) extended forward and slightly DOWNWARD, gauntleted hand grips the sword's leather grip, longsword blade extending downward and toward viewer-LEFT with the tip near hip height. THE SHIELD ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) bent at elbow, round shield up at upper-chest / shoulder height. Calm guard stance. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal. Clean thin black silhouette outline in deep warm brown-black with even weight. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Match the framing of the first input image as closely as possible (vfill ~70%, slight tier-break growth from L1_Base envelope). The new horns on the helm may extend the silhouette UPWARD slightly — that small vertical growth is acceptable. DO NOT zoom in further, DO NOT crop the horns at the top of the canvas, DO NOT shift the figure downward. Clean white background — no scenery, no ground plane, minimal contact shadow, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT leave the face open at L9 — the visor MUST be FULLY CLOSED; no skin, no hair, no eyes, no nose, no mustache, no beard visible above the bevor; DO NOT show ANY warm peachy-tan skin tone above the bevor — the entire upper face must be steel; DO NOT make the visor a half-visor — extend it downward to cover the entire face from forehead to bevor; DO NOT make the eye slit large or open — single narrow horizontal slit; DO NOT add multiple eye slits or vertical slits; DO NOT make the breathing perforations a face grille or honeycomb — single horizontal row of ~5-7 small dark circular holes only; DO NOT make the horns large dragon-style or curling ram-style horns — they are MODEST, upward-curving, sweeping slightly outward and back, ~6 inches tall (large royal horns + central crest arrive at L10); DO NOT add a central crest between the horns at L9 — that arrives at L10; DO NOT make the horns gold or gilt — the horn body is steel; only the small mount disc at each horn base is gold; DO NOT replace the entire helm body with gold — gold at L9 is ONLY the helm circumference band, the horn-mount discs, the shield rim, the gold ring around the boss, and the filigree on pauldrons + tassets; DO NOT make the gold dim, brass-toned, bronze-toned, or yellow-painted — gold MUST render as buttery polished saturated YELLOW GOLD METAL with strong contrast against the cool silver-gray steel; DO NOT use mustard or olive yellow for gold — gold is buttery WARM YELLOW; DO NOT make the cuirass medial ridge engraving gold — it remains tonal-only at L9 (gold cuirass piping arrives at L9.5); DO NOT add a gold cross emblem to the shield face — that arrives at L9.5; DO NOT add gold knuckle-plates on the gauntlets — those arrive at L9.5; DO NOT add a fourth lame to the pauldrons — they remain 3-lame at L9 (4-lame with sculpted lion's-head motif arrives at L10); DO NOT extend the tassets longer than at L8; DO NOT change the round shield to any non-round shape; DO NOT change the boss color to gold — the boss DOME remains cool silver-gray steel (only the gold ring AROUND the boss is added at L9); DO NOT replace the sword leather grip with metal; DO NOT change the sword blade shape; DO NOT make the sword blade gold or partially gold — gold sword arrives at L10; DO NOT shrink the livery skirt band to nothing — it remains visible through the front tasset gap; DO NOT change the trouser color or boot color (boots remain warm-brown leather where the sabatons do not cover); DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT swap the hand holding the sword; DO NOT swap the hand holding the shield; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure (especially do not crop the new horns at the top); DO NOT change the background, add scenery, ground plane, or cast shadows; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, or non-white; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
